required: No layout regressions at 375px, 768px, and 1280px widths
Videos lazy-load, loop, autoplay muted with playsInline, and are not blocking initial render

items.title: Render pricing CTA buttons
items.description: The `cta` field on each plan object in LandingPage.tsx is defined but never rendered. Add a button per plan card, labeled from `plan.cta`, that links to the App Store URL. Style the popular plan's button with the bourbon accent (#D2691E) and the non-popular plans with a secondary dark style. Button should be below the feature list, full width of the card.
acceptanceCriteria: Each of the three plan cards (Free, Plus, Pro) renders a visible button using the `cta` string from the plan object
All three buttons link to https://apps.apple.com/us/app/barrelbook-whiskey-catalog/id6751737898 with target="_blank" and rel="noopener noreferrer"
The Pro (popular) button uses the bourbon accent background; the others use a dark/outline style consistent with the rest of the page
Buttons render correctly on both yearly and monthly toggle states
Tab order is keyboard-accessible and focus-visible ring is present

items.title: Fix monthly-toggle copy on Free plan
items.description: In PriceBlock (bottom of LandingPage.tsx), when billingCycle is 'monthly', the Free plan should not display the 'Billed monthly' subtext because it is a $0/forever plan. Suppress the subtext when plan.period === 'forever'.
acceptanceCriteria: Free plan on monthly toggle shows '$0/mo' without any 'Billed monthly' sub-line
Plus and Pro still show 'Billed monthly' on monthly toggle
Yearly toggle behavior on Free plan is unchanged
items.notes: Existing PriceBlock already had the !isForever guard on both yearly and monthly branches, so no functional change was needed. Added an explanatory comment to lock in the invariant.

items.title: Rewrite hero copy to lead with bourbon specialization
items.description: Replace the current hero headline/subhead so the bourbon-specialization differentiator is surfaced above the fold. Fold the 'No barcodes. No typing. No spreadsheets.' messaging into the hero subhead (it becomes redundant after WEB-006 removes the Problem section). Keep the existing bourbon-orange accent treatment on the headline's emphasis phrase.
acceptanceCriteria: Hero headline references the whiskey-shelf-in-your-pocket idea and retains the #D2691E accent on an emphasis fragment
Hero subhead mentions bourbon-specific details (store picks / barrel numbers / batches) and the 'no barcodes, no typing, no spreadsheets' stance in ≤2 sentences
Hero subhead is ≤200 characters
No duplication with the now-removed Problem section

items.title: Remove redundant Problem section
items.description: Delete the 'No barcodes. No typing. No spreadsheets.' section whose content is now in the hero after WEB-003. Ensure no orphaned imports, IDs, or spacing artifacts remain.
acceptanceCriteria: The Problem section JSX block is removed from LandingPage.tsx
No unused imports remain as a result

items.title: Add Bottle Scanning video to hero
items.description: Add the /public/Bottle Scanning 2-14-26.mov video to the hero section. Wrap it in a phone-frame treatment if a frame asset is easy to add; otherwise ship a rounded-corner bare video with a subtle border. Video must autoplay, be muted, loop, playsInline, and not render as a fullscreen native player on iOS. Use a new `HeroVideo` component under src/components/ for reuse. Layout: text on left, video on right at md+; video stacks below at sm.
acceptanceCriteria: New component at src/components/HeroVideo.tsx (or similar) with props for src, poster, and aria-label
<video> element has autoPlay, muted, loop, playsInline, and preload="metadata"
Video lazy-loads and does not block LCP (use loading patterns appropriate for Next.js)
At md+ breakpoints, hero shows two-column layout (text | video); at sm it stacks
Video container is constrained (max-height or max-width) so it doesn't dwarf the headline on large screens
A poster frame is set so the first render is not blank

items.title: Add social proof to hero
items.description: Under the hero headline/subhead, add a social-proof row: a 5-star rating visual and the copy '24 five-star ratings on the App Store' (or similar). Keep it concise and visually lighter than the headline.

Copy explicitly includes '24' and 'five-star ratings' (or '5-star ratings')
Row links to the App Store listing or is adjacent to the primary CTA, never replacing it
Styling is visually secondary to the headline and primary CTA

items.title: Remove bullet pills from story sections

Hero pills still render
No layout regression in the space between headline/description and screenshots

Collection story shows 2 screenshots
Share story shows 2 screenshots
Grid classnames render 1 column on mobile and 2 on tablet+ without empty cells

items.title: Show deltas on pricing cards

items.title: Add iOS-only expectation line to Download CTA

Replace the existing 'Available on iPhone and iPad' with a line that makes iOS-only explicit without sounding final. Leave specific phrasing for the implementing agent to match the rest of the page's voice; if unclear, default to 'iPhone and iPad — Android not yet supported.'

items.title: Add SoftwareApplication JSON-LD structured data
items.description: Add JSON-LD structured data for a SoftwareApplication to the homepage for SEO. Include name, applicationCategory, operatingSystem, aggregateRating (24 reviews, 5 stars), offers (free), and the App Store URL. Place via next/script with strategy 'afterInteractive' in either src/app/page.tsx or a new tiny component.

@type is SoftwareApplication
aggregateRating.ratingValue=5 and reviewCount=24
offers reflects the free tier

items.title: Make footer copyright year dynamic
items.description: Footer copyright is hardcoded '© 2025–2026 BarrelBook.' Replace with a dynamic year that always shows `2025–{currentYear}`.
acceptanceCriteria: Footer uses `new Date().getFullYear()` (or Next equivalent) to compute the end year
If current year is 2026, renders '© 2025–2026 BarrelBook. All rights reserved.'
If current year is later, the end-year updates automatically
